compiled_state.title: cap-0 reconcile (dry-run/reconcile-check) violates its documented 'no writes' contract — must produce a report without persisting
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
Split from terse-frost-ale (the fsck index.lock exposure is fixed). Two remaining items from the same antipattern (mutating capability reachable from the MCP read/ungated section):

1. reconcile(mode=dry-run|reconcile-check) is cap-0 so allowed under REBAR_MCP_READONLY, but per 9d7c the reconcile pass writes bridge_state/ AND files invariant bug tickets (reconcile.py ~:672 — non-filtered passes file bugs = local CREATE events = store writes). Needs acli + creds to reach the write (fetch happens first), so it could not be experimentally triggered in the sandbox. Verify with a fake/recorded acli, then gate: under _readonly(), cap-0 reconcile must perform NO local store writes (suppress invariant bug-filing + bridge_state writes, or refuse).

2. Audit EVERY read-section MCP tool for write side-effects and add a test matrix asserting a REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1 server performs zero store/git/filesystem mutations for each. Known read-section tools: show_ticket, list_tickets, ticket_deps, ready_tickets, next_batch, search, fsck (fixed), clarity_check, check_ac, quality_check, validate, get_file_impact, get_verify_commands, summary, list_epics, bridge_fsck, reconcile.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Verify (fake acli) whether reconcile dry-run/reconcile-check write the store under readonly
- [ ] If so, gate so cap-0 reconcile under readonly performs no local writes
- [ ] Read-only write-side-effect test matrix across all read-section tools
- [ ] validate / bridge_fsck confirmed side-effect-free under readonly

comments.body:
VERIFIED with real acli (project DIG, navasage.atlassian.net), 2026-06-10 — AC#1 confirmed.

Experiment: isolated temp REBAR_ROOT (git init + rebar init), REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1, build_server(), called the reconcile tool with mode=dry-run (cap-0, so the 9d7c gate ALLOWS it; never called a mutating mode). Snapshotted the store before/after.

Result: the READ-ONLY server WROTE 6 files to the local store:
  - bridge_state/snapshots/<ts>.json            (993 real DIG issues fetched + persisted)
  - bridge_state/snapshots/<ts>.manifest.json
  - bridge_state/health/<ts>.json
  - bridge_state/sync-log-<ts>.jsonl
  - .tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json
  - .tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/prev_snapshot.json
No local ticket dirs were filed on this data (no dual-identity invariant violations triggered the bug-filing path this run), but the bridge_state writes alone confirm the exposure: a REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1 server performs local store writes via the cap-0 reconcile path. dry-run remained cap-0 (zero Jira mutations — confirmed, no issues created/edited on DIG).

So 'dry-run' is not write-free: it persists snapshots/bindings/sync-log/health regardless of mode, and the invariant bug-filing path can create local tickets when violations exist.

Recommended fix (decision needed): EITHER (a) refuse reconcile entirely under REBAR_MCP_READONLY (cleanest — a read-only server should not run the reconciler at all, since even cap-0 modes write bridge_state + can file bug tickets), OR (b) run cap-0 reconcile under readonly in a no-persist mode (suppress bridge_state writes + invariant bug-filing). (a) is simpler and matches 'read-only means no writes'; it changes the documented 'reconcile dry-run is an available read tool' contract.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
CORRECTED FIX DIRECTION (per review): the right behavior is NOT to refuse reconcile under readonly, nor a bespoke MCP path — it's to make cap-0 modes honor the contract the Mode docstring ALREADY states:
    RECONCILE_CHECK -- read-only discrepancy report; no writes
    DRY_RUN         -- read-only analysis; no Jira or ticket writes
reconcile_once already RETURNS the computed mutation plan (the report). The bug is that the persistence side-effects run regardless of mode. So: keep the report, skip ALL local writes when MODE_CAPS[mode]==0.

Write points to gate on a writing-mode (cap!=0) / persist flag in reconcile_once (verified by code read):
  1. SyncLogger init+log+close -> bridge_state/sync-log-<pass>.jsonl   (use a no-op logger when not persisting)
  2. fetcher.fetch_snapshot -> bridge_state/snapshots/<pass>.json      (needs a compute-without-persist path returning the dict)
  3. invariants.check_at_most_one_local_id / check_dual_identity_complete / report_schema_drift -> file local bug tickets (CREATE events) — skip
  4. health.record_pass -> bridge_state/health/<ts>.json               — skip
  5. applier pass manifest -> bridge_state/snapshots/<pass>.manifest.json — skip
  6. binding_store.save() -> .tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json (+ prev_snapshot.json) — skip
prev_snapshot is READ-only (tracker/.bridge_state), so the report stays accurate without persisting curr.

Entanglement: the returned report's mutations_applied is currently read back from the persisted manifest (reconcile.py ~:1150) — a no-persist mode must source the report from the in-memory  list instead.

SCOPE QUESTION: the docstring says dry-run/reconcile-check are no-write PERIOD, so the most-correct fix makes cap-0 no-write EVERYWHERE (CLI + library + MCP), not just under REBAR_MCP_READONLY. That also changes CLI  (currently persists a snapshot). Confirm universal vs MCP-readonly-only before implementing.

Blast radius: reconcile core + 5 helper modules + existing tests that reference dry-run/reconcile-check (test_reconcile_check_bindings_dict.py, test_mode*, test_sync_logger.py, etc.) — do as a dedicated change with full reconciler-suite verification.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
REPORT-FIDELITY CHECK (real acli, project DIG) — answers "does cap-0 still produce a useful report of what a writing mode would do?":

YES, at the differ/plan level (verified):
- Empty local store + DIG (993 issues already rebar-id-labeled) -> outbound_differ total=0, inbound_differ total=0: correctly "in sync, nothing to reconcile".
- Added ONE local-only ticket (no Jira counterpart) -> outbound_differ total=1 create=1: exactly the outbound-create a writing mode would attempt.
cap-0 runs the SAME differ computation a writing mode uses; differ-level dedup (rebar-id label stand-down) IS reflected. So the report faithfully shows "what is out of sync / what would be attempted".

CAVEAT (existing cap-0 semantics, NOT changed by the no-write fix): cap-0 defers all mutations and invokes NO applier leaves, so APPLY-TIME resolution is not exercised — notably outbound creates that would dedup-skip at apply time (matching Jira issue found by summary) still appear as "create" in the report. So the report can slightly OVERSTATE vs the final applied count. It is a discrepancy/plan report, not a guaranteed apply-outcome.

IMPLICATION FOR THE FIX: the high-level report (RECON outbound/inbound differ totals) is already returned to callers via stderr. The DETAILED per-mutation plan is currently written to a bridge_state manifest FILE — which the no-write fix must NOT write. So the fix should RETURN the detailed plan in the result dict instead of persisting it (the data is all in-memory). Net: the no-write change does not reduce report usefulness; it motivates surfacing the report in the return value rather than as a side-effect file.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
